title: malus segurensis
intro: Malus segurensis, also known as the wild apple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Malus segurensis include reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and providing protection against certain diseases. Herbal preparations of Malus segurensis can be made into infusions, tinctures, and extracts for medicinal use, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
